breaking_news.title: TeamPCP Executes Massive Trivy Supply Chain Attack Infecting 1,000+ Cloud Environments
breaking_news.summary:
Cybercriminal group TeamPCP has compromised the widely used Trivy vulnerability scanner, injecting credential-stealing malware into official releases and GitHub Actions workflows. This supply chain attack has triggered a snowball effect, infecting over 1,000 cloud environments and spreading to related projects like LiteLLM and Aqua Security repositories.

The breach forces thousands of organizations to rotate secrets immediately as attackers leverage stolen CI/CD credentials to deploy infostealers and Kubernetes lateral movement toolkits. Security vendors warn that the compromised Docker images and npm packages pose a severe risk to enterprise cloud infrastructure globally.

breaking_news.title: Citrix Urges Immediate Patching for Critical NetScaler Data Leak Vulnerability
breaking_news.summary:
Citrix has issued an urgent security advisory for a critical memory overread flaw in NetScaler ADC and Gateway, tracked as CVE-2026-3055 with a CVSS score of 9.3. This vulnerability allows unauthenticated remote attackers to leak sensitive data from the appliance's memory if configured as a SAML IDP.

Security firms warn that the flaw is poised for active exploitation, potentially exposing authentication tokens and sensitive configuration data across enterprise networks. Organizations relying on Citrix infrastructure for identity management are urged to apply updates immediately to prevent unauthorized data exfiltration.

breaking_news.title: Oracle Patches Critical Unauthenticated RCE Flaw in Identity Manager
breaking_news.summary:
Oracle has released an emergency patch for a critical remote code execution vulnerability in Identity Manager and Web Services Manager, identified as CVE-2026-21992. The flaw carries a maximum CVSS score of 9.8 and can be exploited by unauthenticated attackers over HTTP to gain full system control.

This vulnerability poses a severe risk to enterprise identity infrastructure, potentially allowing threat actors to compromise sensitive user data and administrative functions. Oracle strongly recommends applying the security updates immediately to mitigate the risk of active exploitation in the wild.

breaking_news.title: FBI and CISA Warn of Russian Hackers Hijacking Signal and WhatsApp Accounts
breaking_news.summary:
The FBI and CISA have issued a joint warning about Russian intelligence-linked actors conducting phishing campaigns to hijack Signal and WhatsApp accounts. These attackers pose as support staff to trick high-value targets, including government officials, military personnel, and journalists, into surrendering access credentials.

The campaign aims to bypass end-to-end encryption by stealing account access rather than breaking the encryption itself, enabling surveillance and data theft. Intelligence agencies urge users to verify support communications and enable additional security measures to protect sensitive communications.

breaking_news.title: DarkSword iOS Exploit Leaked Online Threatening 270 Million Devices
breaking_news.summary:
A sophisticated iOS exploit chain known as DarkSword has been leaked online, putting up to 270 million iPhones at risk of compromise. CISA has added the associated vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, ordering federal agencies to patch immediately.

The exploit kit has been observed in cryptocurrency theft and cyberespionage attacks, allowing attackers to bypass security controls and access sensitive data on unpatched devices. Apple users are urged to update their operating systems to the latest version to mitigate the risk of infection.

breaking_news.title: Hackers Exploit Critical Quest KACE Vulnerability for Remote Code Execution
breaking_news.summary:
Threat actors are actively exploiting a maximum-severity security flaw in Quest KACE Systems Management Appliance, tracked as CVE-2025-32975 with a CVSS score of 10.0. Arctic Wolf observed malicious activity targeting unpatched SMA systems exposed to the internet starting in early March.

This vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code on affected systems, potentially leading to full network compromise and data theft. Organizations using Quest KACE infrastructure are advised to apply patches immediately to prevent unauthorized access and lateral movement.

breaking_news.title: North Korean Hackers Abuse VS Code Auto-Run to Spread StoatWaffle Malware
breaking_news.summary:
North Korea-linked threat actor Team 8 is spreading StoatWaffle malware by abusing the auto-run tasks feature in Microsoft Visual Studio Code. Attackers embed malicious configuration files in blockchain-themed project repositories that execute code when developers open and trust the folder.

This campaign marks an evolution in the Contagious Interview threat activity, targeting developers with near-frictionless compromise embedded directly into their workflows. Security researchers warn that detection efforts must expand to include weaponized development environments to counter this stealthy tactic.

breaking_news.title: TeamPCP Deploys CanisterWorm to Wipe Kubernetes Clusters Targeting Iran
breaking_news.summary:
The TeamPCP hacking group is targeting Kubernetes clusters with a malicious script that wipes all machines when it detects systems configured for Iran. This destructive wiper campaign, dubbed CanisterWorm, spreads through poorly secured cloud services and exploits exposed Docker APIs.

The attack represents a financially motivated group attempting to inject itself into geopolitical conflicts by destroying data on systems matching specific time zones or languages. Security experts warn that the self-propagating nature of the worm poses a significant risk to cloud-native infrastructure globally.
